steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a noun phrase ("a new country dialing code") followed by a blank, then another noun phrase ("incoming international telephone calls to reach the country"). We need a word form that can modify a noun without creating a second finite verb in the same clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'had enabled': finite verb (past perfect) — creates a second main verb, making the sentence grammatically broken. ✗
  • Choice B — 'would enable': finite verb (conditional) — same problem as A. ✗
  • Choice C — 'enabled': finite verb (simple past) — same problem. ✗
  • Choice D — 'enabling': present participle — functions as a modifier, attaching to the noun phrase "a new country dialing code" and describing its purpose without adding a second finite verb. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
